prompt: Please build this Figma screen as a new feature in our Angular app: https://www.figma.com/design/zxKV5PL63p3nDkbR62MR03/Material-3-You--Sample-App-with-great-Prototype--Community-?node-id=24-3285&t=SaRBq3tmWNC9JpTy-4

assertions.text: Output explicitly asks the developer for a user story / behavior description before generating finalized code
Output shows evidence the agent successfully called mcp__figma__get_metadata / get_design_context / get_variable_defs and used the returned data (not just claimed it would)
Output correctly identifies the frame as a stacked-card feed (3× stacked-card + FAB + top-app-bar, mobile 411×731), NOT a Profile screen

Output contains a structured audit with actual numbers from the Figma extract: token census (bound vs raw counts from real data), component identification (named real instances like stacked-card), inconsistencies if any
Output contains a numbered list of open questions for the developer to answer before final code generation
Output addresses or at least names each of the five risks: missing tokens, token gaps, inconsistencies, hierarchy, missing states
Output does NOT generate code based on guesses about behavior or invented data when Figma did not specify
Output proposes a feature folder name, route, and component breakdown that fits src/app/features/<feature>/{feature,ui,data,util}, with the ui component derived from the real stacked-card instance
Output explicitly addresses how tablet and desktop should adapt — either by extracting variant frames from Figma or asking the developer (since the visible frame is mobile-only)
Output references --mat-sys-* tokens and shows the gap-closure ritual (// gap-closed: comment) for any tokens Figma uses that lack a Material role match
Output mentions a validation plan: ng lint, ng build, playwright-cli visual checks across mobile/tablet/desktop
